questions.question: What is the topic for Week 41?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Forty-One is STRATEGIC LEADERSHIP.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 40?
questions.explanation: RAHAB was the topic for Week 40.

questions.question: Moreover you shall select from all the people able men, such as fear God, men of truth, hating ________; and place such over them to be rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
questions.explanation: The memory verse (Exodus 18:21) specifies 'men of truth, hating covetousness.'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is Exodus 18:21.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 41 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: Moreover you shall select from all the people able men, such as fear God, men of truth, hating covetousness; and place such over them to be rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of Exodus 18:21 (NKJV).

questions.question: According to the memory verse (Exodus 18:21), what four levels of rulers should be placed over the people?
options: A. Rulers of thousands, hundreds, fifties, and tens
B. Rulers of nations, provinces, cities, and villages
C. Rulers of armies, fleets, priests, and judges
D. Rulers of churches, schools, markets, and courts
questions.explanation: Exodus 18:21 specifies 'rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.'

questions.question: According to the introduction, what does it mean to be 'strategic'?
options: A. To be aggressive and bold in leadership
B. To fix a long-term objective and interest and the means to achieve such
C. To manage people through strict rules and discipline
D. To be politically savvy and network with the right people
questions.explanation: The introduction defines 'strategic' as 'to fix a long-term objective and interest and the means to achieve such.'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'TRAITS OF STRATEGIC LEADERS'.

questions.question: What does the trait 'Allegiance To The Group's Vision' mean for strategic leaders, according to the lesson?
options: A. They enforce the leader's personal agenda above the group
B. They are completely devoted to the group's vision, see the big picture, and map out strategies for its fulfilment
C. They demand absolute loyalty from all followers
D. They vote on every major decision together as a team
questions.explanation: Outline I states 'Strategic leaders are completely devoted to the group's vision; they see the big picture and map out strategies to see to its fulfilment (Proverbs 29:18a; Habakkuk 2:2).'

questions.question: Which scriptures are cited under the trait 'Allegiance To The Group's Vision'?
questions.explanation: Outline I cites '(Proverbs 29:18a; Habakkuk 2:2)' alongside the trait of Allegiance To The Group's Vision.

questions.question: 'Cautious Use Of Power' means strategic leaders oppress their subordinates and rule them with fear.
questions.explanation: Outline I states 'As leaders, they do not oppress their subordinates. They lead with the fear of God (Ephesians 6:9).'

questions.question: The trait 'Delegation' in strategic leaders involves what specific purpose, according to the lesson?
options: A. Keeping all authority to themselves to avoid errors
B. Giving assignments and transferring power to subordinates and deliberately raising future leaders
C. Rotating leadership monthly among team members
D. Reporting everything upward to a higher authority
questions.explanation: Outline I states they 'give assignments and transfer power to their subordinates' and 'deliberately raise their followers to be future leaders (2 Timothy 2:2).'

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'WAYS TO BE ONE'.

questions.question: According to Outline II, what does 'widening your scope' as a strategic leader mean?
options: A. Expanding your personal wealth and influence
B. Thinking of the progress of the whole organisation instead of limiting success to yourself and your unit
C. Growing your team size as rapidly as possible
D. Acquiring more resources exclusively for your own unit
questions.explanation: Outline II states 'Widen your scope by thinking of the progress of the whole organisation instead of limiting the success level to yourself and your unit (Nehemiah 2:3-5).'

questions.question: According to the lesson, it takes humility to lead strategically (Proverbs 22:4).
questions.explanation: Outline II concludes 'It takes humility to lead strategically (Proverbs 22:4).'

questions.question: According to Outline II, what is described as 'key to strategic leadership'?
questions.explanation: Outline II states 'Knowledge is key to strategic leadership, so, keep yourself abreast with developments within and outside your environment (Proverbs 18:15).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support exemplifying good values so you can lead your followers in the right way?
questions.explanation: Outline II states 'Exemplify good values so you can lead your followers in the right way (1 Corinthians 11:1).'

questions.question: According to the lesson, a strategic leader should recognise and seize opportunities all the time (Numbers 13:30).
questions.explanation: Outline II states 'Recognise and seize opportunities all the time (Numbers 13:30).'

questions.question: According to the conclusion, what is the essence of being a strategic leader?
options: A. To control all decisions from the top
B. To look beyond the surface into the big picture
C. To gather the most resources for your team
D. To ensure your unit outperforms all others
questions.explanation: The conclusion states 'To be a strategic leader is to look beyond the surface into the big picture.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Come and let us build the wall of Jerusalem, that we may no longer be a reproach.'
questions.explanation: This phrase is from verse 17 of the Bible Passage (Nehemiah 2:17-18).

questions.question: According to Nehemiah 2:17, what was the state of Jerusalem and its gates?
options: A. Jerusalem was thriving and its gates were newly built
B. Jerusalem was under siege by the Assyrians
C. Jerusalem lay waste and its gates were burned with fire
D. Jerusalem was abandoned and its walls were intact
questions.explanation: Nehemiah 2:17 states 'You see the distress that we are in, how Jerusalem lies waste, and its gates are burned with fire.'

questions.question: According to Nehemiah 2:18, what two things did Nehemiah tell the people to inspire them to build?
options: A. The history of Jerusalem and the prophecy of Isaiah
B. The hand of God which had been good upon him, and the king's words that he had spoken to him
C. The wealth available for the project and the size of their enemies
D. The detailed construction plans and the expected timeline
questions.explanation: Nehemiah 2:18 states 'I told them of the hand of my God which had been good upon me, and also of the king's words that he had spoken to me.'

questions.question: The trait 'Empathy' in strategic leaders means what, according to the lesson?
options: A. Imposing strict rules without exception to maintain discipline
B. Leading with compassion, recognising that leaders are also humans with strengths and weaknesses
C. Making all decisions based purely on data and logic
D. Delegating all emotional issues to other team members
questions.explanation: Outline I states 'They lead with compassion recognising that though they are leaders, they are also humans with their strengths and weaknesses (Ephesians 4:32).'

questions.question: The trait 'Transparency' in strategic leaders is described as what?
options: A. Leaders share all classified information with the public
B. Leaders make every decision by committee vote
C. People can easily see through them as they carry out their day-to-day activities without any form of dishonesty
D. Leaders submit weekly accountability reports to their followers
questions.explanation: Outline I states 'People can easily see through them as they carry out their day-to-day activities without any form of dishonesty (Psalm 26:11a; Proverbs 11:3).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited under the trait 'Delegation'?
questions.explanation: Outline I cites '(2 Timothy 2:2)' alongside the Delegation trait, concerning raising followers to be future leaders.

questions.question: They synergise with their subordinates and allow for a free flow of ________. They seek to understand and to be understood (Proverbs 25:11; Colossians 4:6).
questions.explanation: Outline I describes the trait 'Effective Communication' as allowing for 'a free flow of communication.'

questions.question: Being creative and confident to bring new initiatives is linked in the lesson to being sensitive to change (2 Chronicles 26:10, 14-15).
questions.explanation: Outline II states 'Always be creative and confident to bring new initiatives to the company (2 Chronicles 26:10, 14-15)' in the same sentence about being sensitive to change.

questions.question: Which scriptures are cited to support increasing your value to the group by contributing effectively to its growth?
questions.explanation: Outline II states 'Increase your value to the group by contributing effectively to its growth (Daniel 6:28; Proverbs 22:29).'

questions.question: Which scriptures are cited under the trait 'Problem-solving Skills'?
questions.explanation: Outline I cites '(Genesis 41:33-36; 1 Samuel 30:20-23)' alongside the trait 'Problem-solving Skills'.

questions.question: According to the introduction, what role does a strategic leader play regarding the group's vision?
options: A. To make all decisions unilaterally without input from others
B. To create the group's overall vision and the means to achieve it, motivating others to foster unity and bring about change
C. To represent the group in all legal and governmental matters
D. To ensure the group never changes its founding methods
questions.explanation: The introduction states 'Strategic leadership is all-inclusive as it creates the group's overall vision and the means to achieve it. Based on this vision, strategic leaders motivate others to foster unity and a way to bring about change.'
